primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivables for hedge accounting designation in consolidated financial statements for foreign currency risk

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 restricts hedge accounting to external party transactions, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides an explicit exception permitting intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements when FX gains/losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

supporting_authority.reason: Provides guidance on hedge accounting for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements, specifying that only FX differences between functional currencies of foreign operations and parent entities qualify, and confirms that hedging instruments may be held by any entity within the group.

Defines foreign operations and establishes the framework for translating foreign operation results into presentation currency, which determines whether FX differences on intragroup monetary items are eliminated on consolidation.

peripheral_authority.reason: While relevant to consolidation generally, does not specifically address hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items.
Addresses classification of financial instruments but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility.
Addresses measurement and recognition of dividend payables but does not address hedge accounting for dividend receivables.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting designation of intragroup dividend receivable as hedged item for FX risk
Recognition of FX gains/losses on monetary item without hedge accounting designation

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on intragroup dividend receivable
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 explicitly permits intragroup monetary items to be designated as hedged items for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements, provided the FX exposure is not fully eliminated on consolidation. A dividend receivable is a recognized monetary asset that can qualify if the FX differences affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Foreign exchange treatment without hedge accounting designation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If hedge accounting is not designated, the dividend receivable is translated at each reporting date with FX differences recognized in profit or loss (or OCI for net investment hedge purposes) under IAS 21, without the specific hedge accounting requirements of IFRS 9.
